rationale: The two notes indicate strong conceptual overlap: the citing paper centers on in-store interventions that raise spending via consumption-goal completion, while the cited paper shows a retail intervention affecting purchases/spending via consumption-process visualization and is moderated by goal specificity. Given this shared mechanism-and-boundary-condition space in in-store shopper behavior (despite only DOI-level match evidence), this is plausibly more than an incidental background mention and should be reviewed in stage two.
